# terrible_azure_azcollection_azure_rm_monitordatacollectionrules (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the data collection rule you're creating/changing
- `resource_group` (String) The name of the resource group

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `data_collection_endpoint_id` (String) The resource ID of the data collection endpoint that this rule can be used with
- `data_flows` (String) Definition of which streams are sent to which destinations. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-mgmt-monitor/azure.mgmt.monitor.v2022_06_01.models.dataflow?view=azure-python
- `data_sources` (String) The specification of data sources. This property is optional and can be omitted if the rule is meant to be used via direct calls to the provisioned endpoint. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-mgmt-monitor/azure.mgmt.monitor.v2022_06_01.models.datacollectionruledatasources?view=azure-python
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description for the data collection rule
- `destinations` (String) https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-mgmt-monitor/azure.mgmt.monitor.v2022_06_01.models.datacollectionruledestinations?view=azure-python
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `kind` (String) Kind of the data collection rule Use `Linux` for Linux. Use `Windows` for Windows.
- `location` (String) Location of the data colelction rule defaults to location of exiting data collection rule or location of the resource group if unspecified
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) State of the data collection rule Use `present` for creating/updating a data collection rule. Use `absent` for deleting a data collection rule.
- `stream_declarations` (String) Declaration of a custom stream. Sub dict is a list of columns used by data in this stream. top level key is the name of the stream_declaration https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-mgmt-monitor/azure.mgmt.monitor.v2022_06_01.models.streamdeclaration?view=azure-python
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `datacollectionrule` (String) Details of the data collection rule Is null on state==absent (data collection rule does not exist or will be deleted) Assumes you make legal changes in check mode
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
